content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes, where the ithi^{th}ith node has a value AiA_iAi‚Äã associated to it.

You need to answer QQQ queries. Each query is of the form:


XXX YYY : Given two nodes XXX and YYY (1‚â§X,Y‚â§N)(1\le X,Y\le N)(1‚â§X,Y‚â§N), find the length of the **longest** arithmetic progression formed using the values of nodes on the shortest path between XXX and YYY.

More formally, let there be MMM nodes on the shortest path between nodes XXX and YYY, and GGG denote the shortest path from node XXX to node YYY, where G1=XG_1 = XG1‚Äã=X, GM=YG_M = YGM‚Äã=Y, and there exists an edge between nodes GiG_iGi‚Äã and Gi+1G_{i+1}Gi+1‚Äã for all (1‚â§i<M)(1 \leq i < M)(1‚â§i<M).

Let BBB denote the array [AG1,AG2,‚Ä¶,AGM][A_{G_1} , A_{G_2}, \dots, A_{G_M}][AG1‚Äã‚Äã,AG2‚Äã‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AGM‚Äã‚Äã]. Find the length of the **longest** subarray of BBB which is an **arithmetic progression**.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer NNN, denoting the number of nodes in the tree.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, the values assigned to each node.
Next N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains 222 space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting edge between nodes uuu and vvv.
Next line contains QQQ, the number of queries.
Next QQQ lines contain 222 space-separated integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each query, print the length of the **longest** arithmetic progression formed using the values of nodes on the shortest path between XXX and YYY.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The tree looks like:

Query 111: The shortest path between nodes 999 and 101010 is [9,5,4,1,2,3,7,10][9,5,4,1,2,3,7,10][9,5,4,1,2,3,7,10]. The corresponding array B=[A9,A5,A4,A1,A2,A3,A7,A10]=[6,5,3,1,3,6,5,4]B = [A_9, A_5, A_4, A_1, A_2, A_3, A_7, A_{10}] = [6, 5, 3, 1, 3, 6, 5, 4]B=[A9‚Äã,A5‚Äã,A4‚Äã,A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã,A7‚Äã,A10‚Äã]=[6,5,3,1,3,6,5,4].
The longest subarray of BBB which is also an arithmetic progression are [5,3,1][5, 3, 1][5,3,1] and [6,5,4][6, 5, 4][6,5,4].
Thus, the length of the longest subarray is 333.
Query 222: The shortest path between nodes 666 and 555 is [6,3,2,1,4,5][6, 3, 2, 1, 4, 5][6,3,2,1,4,5]. The corresponding array B=[A6,A3,A2,A1,A4,A5]=[4,6,3,1,3,5]B = [A_6, A_3, A_2, A_1, A_4, A_5] = [4, 6, 3, 1, 3, 5]B=[A6‚Äã,A3‚Äã,A2‚Äã,A1‚Äã,A4‚Äã,A5‚Äã]=[4,6,3,1,3,5].
The longest subarray of BBB which is also an arithmetic progression is [1,3,5][1, 3, 5][1,3,5].
Thus, the length of the longest subarray is 333.
